findings.title: Reward accounting mixes deposit amounts with LP shares, enabling reward theft and claim lockups

best_agent_match.title: Small deposits can mint zero LP shares and become permanently stuck

findings.title: LP shares are minted from `tokenAmount + ethAmount`, so inventory shifts can overmint shares and drain the scarcer asset

best_agent_match.title: Small deposits can mint zero LP shares and become permanently stuck

findings.title: Deposits use manipulable Uniswap spot reserves instead of the pool's own balances

best_agent_match.title: Orderbook settlement over-credits taxed tokens and can poison pool solvency

findings.title: Fee-on-transfer or deflationary tokens are over-credited, creating unbacked LP balances

best_agent_match.title: Rebasing or confiscatory tokens can desync stored balances and brick exits

findings.title: Small deposits can mint zero LP shares and become permanently stuck

findings.title: Rebasing or confiscatory tokens can desynchronize accounting and lock LP exits

best_agent_match.title: Rebasing or confiscatory tokens can desync stored balances and brick exits

rejected_candidates.title: Orderbook settlement over-credits taxed tokens and can poison pool solvency
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of existing finding `F-004`, which already covers over-crediting on both `provideLiquidity` and `receiveTokenFromOrderbook`.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner emergency withdrawal is an unbounded rug lever that leaves LP state insolvent
rejected_candidates.reason: `emergencyWithdraw` / `emergencyWithdrawETH` are explicit `onlyOwner` escape hatches; absent a contrary trust model, this is an acknowledged privileged capability rather than a code vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: `minTokenAmount` does not provide the advertised slippage protection
rejected_candidates.reason: The parameter is indeed redundant and misleading, but by itself it does not create a distinct protocol-level loss or lockup beyond the already-reported reserve-manipulation issue in `F-003`.
